findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Data Exfiltration via Hardcoded Endpoint and API Key
findings.description: The script cloud_sync.py contains a hardcoded, base64-encoded API endpoint and authentication key. It collects sensitive credentials (AWS, GCP, SSH keys, npm/pypi tokens) and environment variables, then sends them to the external endpoint via an HTTP POST request. This constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration.
findings.evidence: Hardcoded endpoint decoded to 'https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup' and key 'cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ'. Script collects ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.npmrc, etc. and pushes them via urllib.request.urlopen.
findings.remediation: Remove the script entirely. Do not use skills that collect and transmit credentials to external servers. Verify the skill's purpose and code before execution.

findings.title: Credential Theft from Multiple Sensitive Files
findings.description: The script explicitly reads and collects credentials from AWS (~/.aws/credentials), GCP (~/.config/gcloud/...), SSH private keys (~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519), npm registry tokens (~/.npmrc), and Python package registry tokens (~/.pypirc). These are bundled into the snapshot and exfiltrated.
findings.remediation: Never use skills that access credential files. Audit all skills for file access patterns before execution.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Name and Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'cloud-sync' and described as a configuration synchronization utility, but its actual behavior is to collect sensitive credentials and environment data and exfiltrate them to an external server. The name and description are misleading to evade detection.
findings.evidence: Name: 'cloud-sync', Description: 'Synchronize local project configurations to a designated cloud endpoint for backup, restore, and environment reproducibility.' Actual behavior: credential theft and data exfiltration.
findings.remediation: Flag this skill as malicious. The deceptive naming is a red flag for social engineering.

findings.title: Detection Evasion via Base64 Encoding of Malicious Payload
findings.description: The script uses base64 encoding to hide the API endpoint URL and authentication key, making static analysis more difficult. This is a deliberate obfuscation technique to evade security review.
findings.remediation: Do not trust skills that use obfuscation. Require clear, readable code for all skill scripts.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Unauthorized Tool Use and Data Exfiltration
findings.description: The skill's manifest does not declare allowed-tools, but the script uses Python to make network calls (urllib.request) and read sensitive files. This violates the principle of least privilege and constitutes tool poisoning by using Python for malicious purposes.
findings.evidence: Script uses urllib.request.urlopen() for network calls and open() for file reads, without any declared tool restrictions.
findings.remediation: Always specify allowed-tools in the manifest and audit scripts to ensure they comply. Block skills that make unauthorized network calls.

findings.title: Indirect Prompt Injection via External Endpoint
findings.description: The skill pushes data to an external endpoint and could potentially pull instructions or commands from that endpoint during restore operations. The restore command is documented but not implemented in the provided script, suggesting the external endpoint could serve malicious instructions.
findings.evidence: The 'restore' command is listed in the table but not implemented in cloud_sync.py. This suggests the external endpoint may be used to deliver instructions.
findings.remediation: Do not use skills that communicate with external endpoints without clear, auditable code for all operations.

findings.title: Misleading Instructions for Harmful Activity
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructions describe a legitimate backup utility, but the actual script performs credential theft and data exfiltration. The instructions are designed to deceive the user and the agent into executing malicious code.
findings.evidence: Instructions describe 'Environment configuration backup and cross-machine synchronization utility' but script exfiltrates credentials.
findings.remediation: Verify skill behavior matches documentation. Be suspicious of skills that claim to access credentials for 'backup' purposes.
